content.answer:
Hi Ian and all,

Happy New Year!
Please find the attachment for Jan 7 KR/JP bi-weekly meeting.
[image: image.png]
Thank you.

Best

*Junho Kim, 김준호*

Shopee | KR/JP

*Mobile:* +82 10 3247 2

*Address:* Centerfield West 16F, 231 Teheran-ro, Seoul, Korea

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - What happened to Cosrx in VN?
What is their operating model exactly?
- How will management of this brand be moving forward?

Slide 9

   - Do we have a LFF view for 2026 and how will it look like?
- How has ADO in TH been impacted with the recent tax increase?

Slide 12

   - Torridien >> NPC continues to be an issue?
- What is their feedback on joining local OS?

Slide 17

   - TH situation looks not good?

Slide 19 – 20

   - On campaign participation I cannot tell % of actual
   - How about campaign participation in all other markets?

I did not hear
   back. It seems that it is an issue in TH and TW?

Others

   - What are the key projects that we are working on right now?

Is there a
   priority list to go through and status?

- I remember we wanted to discuss RM KPIs for 2026, where did we land on
   that?
What are we working on right now?

- I remember we wanted to have a seller awards event in KR, what is the
   latest on that?
- What is the latest on the F&B acquisition and SLS integration for TW?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,
please find the answers to your questions regarding the 2026 LFF target.

*Follow Up Question 1: Sharing recap of 2026 LFF bottom up projection
calculation methodology*

A: The calculation logic for 2026 LFF target projection is as follows:
LFF 2026 ADO Projection = Existing LFF SKU ADO + New LFF SKU ADO
New LFF SKU ADO = Total Addressable New LFF SKUs * Historical SLS to LFF
CVR% * Target SKU Efficiency









*Note: *Existing SKU ADO = Existing SKU# * Target SKU efficiency*New SKU
ADO = New SKU# * Target SKU efficiency*New SKU# = Addressable SKU# *
Conversion %*SKU# is model level*Existing SKU = SKUs inbounded before ‘26
Jan 1*New SKU = SKUs inbounding starting ‘26 Jan 1*Addressable SKU# = # of
managed brands’ SLS SKU with  ADI>=0.5*Existing SKU# is projected based on
historical SKU# depreciation trends and new SKU# is projected based on
target conversion % from the addressable SKU pool, which is managed brands’
SLS SKUs with ADI ≥ 0.5. Each existing and new target ADO is projected by
multiplying the respective target SKU# by the corresponding target SKU
efficiency.*

*Follow Up Question 2: Review 2026 TH LFF target (consider total LFF
eligible shops and SKUs based on current ADIS, only including managed brand
sellers), new SLS to LFF SKU CVR% projection given TH SLS tax hike
(benchmarking SLS to LFF SKU CVR change when SLS tax was implemented in
other markets)*

[image: image.png]
A: We adjusted 2026 Dec TH LFF target ADO from 2,745 to 3,442 (TH LFF%
increased by 9%p, increasing 2026 total LFF% by 1%p). The order projection
increase is expected to come from new LFF SKUs, as we increased the target
SLS SKU conversion rate from 16% to 32%, benchmarking MY’s SLS SKU
conversion rate growth at 2x after the '24 LVG tax hike from SLS.

*Follow Up Question 3: Check TH 2026 FBS space capacity and whether TH
needs to lower down ADIS (also check whether there is meaningful ADO if we
do lower down ADIS)*

A: TH 2026 FB
